rationale.R1.2: The section chains calibration improvements → confident-regime accuracy → clinical utility; reviewing whether this logical arc holds (e.g., ECE reduction does not by itself guarantee the 92.4% high-confidence claim) is a standard expectation of any substantive review.
rationale.R1.3: The section is quantitatively dense: six AUROC pairs, ECE averages, a high-confidence accuracy claim, and a forward-pointer to reliability diagrams. Verifying that every numerical assertion is traceable to a stated experiment or table is load-bearing — unsupported figures in this position would directly undermine the section's credibility.
rationale.R1.4: An explicit, hedged novelty claim ('We are unaware of any prior method that achieves comparable calibration on the ClinVar-2024 split at the same parameter budget') appears verbatim. Assessing whether comparator scope is fairly drawn (parameter-budget qualifier, single split) is central to a responsible review.
rationale.R1.6: General 'review' instructions canonically include prose quality; the section contains phrasing that may benefit from tightening (e.g., the final sentence's scope broadens abruptly to 'population scale').

parsed.clarification_question: Does 'review' here mean (a) editorial review — prose clarity, flow, and style are the primary deliverable — or (b) substantive scientific review — logical validity, evidence-claim fidelity, and novelty assessment are the primary deliverable?
